commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an internal Feishu docx block-list pagination helper, routes docx read/list_blocks/clear paths through it, and adds a regression test for two-page list_blocks aggregation.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +44. Total +50 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source-contract level: current main and v2026.6.8 call documentBlock.list once while the locked SDK exposes paginated page_token/has_more fields. I did not establish a live Feishu tenant reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is live Feishu proof or maintainer acceptance of local contract proof, which is not a narrow code repair lane for automation.

target.title: fix(feishu): paginate docx block listing
target.body:
status: 📣 needs proof — local root-cause proof supplied; live Feishu tenant proof is not available in this environment

## Summary

- Problem: Feishu docx still called `documentBlock.list` once, so documents with more than one block page could omit later blocks from `list_blocks`, `read` block counts/type hints, and overwrite cleanup.
- Solution: Add one internal docx block-list pagination helper and route the docx block-list consumers through it.
- What changed: `read`, `list_blocks`, and document clearing now follow `has_more` / `page_token` until Feishu reports the terminal page.
- What did NOT change: No public tool schema, public response shape, channel delivery path, provider routing, session state, storage format, or Feishu account configuration changed.
- Why it matters / User impact: Large Feishu documents can span multiple `documentBlock.list` pages; returning or processing only page one makes OpenClaw omit later content and gives maintainers misleading block counts.
- Fixes #93796 follow-up

## Origin / follow-up

- **Follows / completes:** #93796 fixed the same Feishu pagination class for wiki space and wiki node listing.
- **Gap this fills:** Feishu docx block listing still missed the same continuation-token contract on `documentBlock.list`.
- **Consistency:** This patch applies the same source pagination rule: read items, check `has_more`, pass the returned `page_token`, and stop at the terminal page.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked PR(s):** N/A — no open PR collision found for `feishu docx pagination documentBlock list` before submission.
- **Gap in linked PR(s):** N/A.
- **Why this patch is better/canonical:** The fix lives at the Feishu docx block-list source-of-truth helper instead of adding downstream special cases to individual actions.
- **Local real behavior proof advantage:** The regression drives the public `feishu_doc` `list_blocks` action through the local tool execution path and verifies both requested page tokens and merged blocks.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Feishu docx block listing now follows `documentBlock.list` continuation pages instead of returning only the first page.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw Feishu extension tool execution path on Linux, exercising `registerFeishuDocTools` and the `feishu_doc` `list_blocks` action through the repository test harness after syncing onto current `origin/main`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-94632
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/docx.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-94632

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
   Start at  10:19:18
   Duration  23.64s (transform 18.32s, setup 204ms, import 22.86s, tests 59ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 39.69s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The regression test drives two Feishu block-list pages and observes both pages returned by `feishu_doc` `list_blocks`.
- **What was not tested:** A live Feishu tenant/API call was not run because this environment has no Feishu/Lark env vars and the local OpenClaw config does not include `channels.feishu`; maintainers can either replay with a real multi-page Feishu document or explicitly accept the local contract proof for this owner-module fix.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Maintainer decision needed — ClawSweeper requested redacted live Feishu tenant/API proof, but no Feishu credentials or Feishu channel config are available in this environment.
- **RF-002:** Maintainer decision needed — the local proof remains the public tool-path regression plus SDK continuation-token contract coverage; live multi-page Feishu tenant replay still needs maintainer-side credentials or explicit acceptance of local-only proof.
- **RF-003:** Maintainer decision needed — this PR still cannot attach redacted live Feishu output from this environment; the PR body now states that limitation directly in `What was not tested`.
- **RF-004:** Disclosed — the merge-risk section names the availability/rate-limit concern from sequential Feishu page fan-out and keeps the implementation scoped to the owner module without adding hidden retry/fallback behavior.
- **RF-005:** Maintainer decision needed — ClawSweeper found no narrow code repair lane, so the remaining path is live Feishu proof from a credentialed setup or maintainer acceptance of the local contract proof.
- **CI-001:** `security-fast` failure attribution: the failing undici advisory was from the stale base, not this PR patch. After `sync-main`, the PR diff is again limited to `extensions/feishu/src/docx.ts` and `extensions/feishu/src/docx.test.ts`; dependency files come from current `origin/main`.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/feishu/src/docx.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** `list_blocks follows Feishu document block pagination` sets up page one with `has_more: true` and `page_token: "page-2"`, then verifies the second request and the combined block result.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The test enters through the public `feishu_doc` action that previously exposed the bug, while keeping the external Feishu API boundary local and deterministic.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** compatibility, availability.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch touches the Feishu `documentBlock.list` pagination contract and can make one docx tool invocation perform multiple sequential Feishu API calls; it does not change OpenClaw's public schema, defaults, response shape, or caller-facing compatibility.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is limited to one owner module and one regression test, follows the Feishu continuation-token contract, and leaves live Feishu tenant latency/rate-limit replay for maintainer-side verification if required.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root cause was that Feishu docx had three direct `documentBlock.list` call sites that did not pass a page size or follow `has_more` / `page_token`, so the owner module could silently treat the first page as the complete block set.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The root-cause path is the docx block-list source of truth; centralizing it in one pagination helper means every current docx block-list consumer gets all pages before any downstream read/count/delete behavior runs.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium — the code path and local public-tool regression are clear after syncing onto current main, but ClawSweeper asked for live Feishu tenant proof that is unavailable in this environment.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch quality warning considered: `documentBlock.list` is a public Feishu SDK contract term, but this PR only consumes that contract correctly; it is not a fallback, catch-all guard, or symptom mask.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source of truth is `extensions/feishu/src/docx.ts` for Feishu docx block listing. Compatibility remains unchanged, related open PR scan found no same-contract follow-up, and out of scope are public schema changes, new user parameters, live Feishu credentials, and non-docx Feishu tools.
target.author: mushuiyu886
